subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1** board map of Nordic countries (Denmark, Finland, Norway, Sweden)
- **120** colored train cars (40 per player in 3 colors)
- **110** train car cards (12 each of 8 colors + 14 locomotives)
- **46** destination ticket cards
- **3** wooden scoring markers
- **1** Globetrotter bonus card (most completed tickets)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place board** showing the Nordic map.
2. **Shuffle train car cards** — Deal 5 face-up. Place rest as draw pile.
3. **Shuffle destination tickets** separately.
4. **Place Globetrotter card** beside the board.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Choose color** — Take 40 train cars in your color.
- **Deal 4 train car cards** to each player.
- **Deal 3 destination tickets** — Keep at least 2.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Ticket to Ride: Nordic Countries is a standalone 2-3 player version featuring tunnels and ferries across Scandinavia. Same core mechanics as Ticket to Ride with tighter competition.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn, do ONE action:
1. **Draw Train Car Cards** — Draw 2 cards (face-up locomotive counts as both draws).
2. **Claim a Route** — Play matching cards to place trains.
3. **Draw Destination Tickets** — Draw 3, keep at least 1.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Tunnels
- Marked with special border. After playing cards, flip 3 from draw pile.
- Each matching color or locomotive revealed costs 1 extra card.
- If you can't or won't pay, take your cards back.

#### Ferries
- Routes with locomotive icons require that many locomotive cards.

#### Route Scoring
- 1 car: 1 pt, 2 cars: 2 pts, 3 cars: 4 pts, 4 cars: 7 pts, 5 cars: 10 pts, 6 cars: 15 pts, 9 cars: 27 pts.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **No double routes** — In 2-player games, both routes between cities may be claimed.
- **Tight map** — With only 40 trains per player, route selection is critical.
- **Locomotive limit** — Face-up locomotive = entire draw action.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Trigger
- When any player has 2 or fewer trains left, everyone gets 1 final turn.

#### Scoring
- Route points (during game) + completed tickets − failed tickets.
- **Globetrotter bonus** — 10 points for the player who completed the most destination tickets.

#### Winner
- Highest score wins. Ties broken by Globetrotter, then most completed tickets.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Tight train supply** — With only 40 trains, every route matters.
- **Tunnels are risky** — Keep extra cards for tunnel attempts.
- **Collect locomotives** — They're essential for ferries and flexible for tunnels.
- **Block aggressively** — In 2-3 players, blocking is very effective.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Ticket volume** — The Globetrotter bonus rewards completing many short tickets.
- **Northern routes** — Long northern routes are high-value but risky tunnels.
- **Card efficiency** — Prioritize routes that complete multiple tickets.
- **Deny key connections** — Block opponents at chokepoints between regions.
